name: Water electrolysis
notes: Largest single load; splits water to H2+O2

name: ISRU control & autonomy
notes: Unattended process control under comms delay

name: Water processing & melt
notes: Rodwell melt or process to electrolysis-grade

name: Surface relay & ground station
notes: Link base to Earth across delay

name: Precision-landing beacons
notes: Let follow-on ships cluster within meters

name: Central compute & autonomy
notes: Rad-tolerant compute; fault mgmt; digital twin

name: Solar array module (1000 m2)
notes: 24h-avg electrical ~30 kW per 1000 m2; dies in dust storms

name: Kinetic penetrator survey set
notes: Dozens of steel/tungsten rods released on approach (~8 km/s); craters surveyed from orbit. JPL tungsten-ballast impact precedent. Manifest mass; never soft-lands (see docs/CONSIDERED.md)

name: Orbital radar/relay constellation (12 sats)
notes: Marslink-class phased-array sats deployed from aerobraking ship; multistatic subsurface radar + relay (Handmer Feb-2025). Orbital asset counted as delivered mass (simplification logged)

name: Regolith refinery (metals/ceramics/glass)
notes: Gen-1 closure: crude alloys ceramics glass from regolith (arxiv 1612.03238 ladder). Notional D-tier

name: Polymer & chemicals plant
notes: Gen-2.5 closure: plastics rubbers chemicals from CO2/H2O feedstock. Notional D-tier

name: Agriculture module (200 m2 growing)
notes: ~200 m2 stacked growing area feeds ~4 people (BVAD 50 m2/person planning value). Lighting-dominated load (~400 W/m2); crops die in a blackout -> critical

name: Electronics fabrication module
notes: Gen-4 closure: boards/components locally; chips still imported. Notional D-tier

name: Semiconductor fab (local lithography)
notes: Gen-5 closure: the last import to die (arxiv 1612.03238 -- incomplete chip closure grows imports exponentially). Notional D-tier

name: Inflatable habitat (stowed)
notes: ~300 m3 deployed from ~75 m3 stowed (~4x, TransHab/B330-class inflatables; TransHab 329.4 m3 deployed per BVAD). Fixes the habitat_module stowed=deployed simplification at city scale (docs/CONSIDERED.md)
